sections.content:
Decision records in this package use their `tags` field as topic labels for browsing decisions by topic (user story S3: browse by date and topic).

Each tag is a short noun phrase identifying the topic area of the decision (e.g. `finance`, `space`, `governance`, `artists`, `branding`). Tags are free-form strings; no closed vocabulary is enforced in this package version. Future packages may install a Vocabulary (RFC-006) to curate the tag set.

Multi-topic decisions may carry multiple tags. When filtering by topic in a UI or via CLI, match on `tags` containing the chosen topic key (`srs find --tag <topic>`).
sections.label: Convention
sections.name: convention

sections.content:
A decision about exhibition insurance -> tags: ["artists", "finance"]
A decision about wall mounting standards -> tags: ["space", "installation"]
A governance decision about meeting quorum -> tags: ["governance"]
sections.label: Examples
sections.name: examples

sections.content: The decision-log DocumentView hides retired decisions by default: its section source sets `excludeLifecycleStates: ["superseded", "closed", "abandoned"]`. A default render therefore shows only live (draft/proposed/ratified) decisions; a show-all client drops the exclusion (see `container resolve-view`, which returns `excludeLifecycleStates` and per-member `isVisibleByDefault`).
sections.label: Lifecycle visibility
sections.name: lifecycle-visibility
